1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: ADR-027
4	  depends_on:
5	    - FEAT-011
6	    - FEAT-015
7	    - ADR-003
8	---
9	# ADR-027: Skill Install Topology and Resolution Precedence
10	
11	**Status:** Accepted
12	**Date:** 2026-06-01
13	
14	## Context
15	
16	DDx ships the `ddx` skill as a package, and operators need to know which
17	install layer they are actually using. Before this decision, the install
18	topology was spread across implementation details and a handful of docs:
19	
20	- the project-local package install under `<project>/.ddx/plugins/ddx/`;
21	- the global fallback under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`;
22	- the baked-in default package embedded in the binary;
23	- the agent-facing skill outputs under `.agents/skills/` and
24	  `.claude/skills/`.
25	
26	Without one documented precedence rule, `ddx doctor` could not tell whether a
27	project was using its own package copy or falling through to the global layer,
28	and the agent docs had no single place to describe where the skill surfaces
29	live.
30	
31	## Decision
32	
33	DDx resolves the default `ddx` package in this order:
34	
35	1. project-local `<project>/.ddx/plugins/ddx/`
36	2. global `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`
37	3. baked-in package embedded in the binary
38	
39	The project-local package is the authoritative install for a repository. The
40	global layer is a fallback and may be used when the project copy is absent.
41	The baked-in layer exists only for the default `ddx` package so the binary
42	remains usable offline.
43	
44	DDx supports two install modes with distinct agent-tier outputs:
45	
46	**Project-local (default, `ddx install <name>`):**
47	- Plugin content: `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` (in-tree) or
48	  `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<identity>/plugins/<name>/` (convention mode)
49	- Agent-tier skill outputs: `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>/` and
50	  `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>/`
51	
52	**Global (`ddx install <name> --global`):**
53	- Plugin content: `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/`
54	- Agent-tier skill outputs: `~/.agents/skills/<name>/` and
55	  `~/.claude/skills/<name>/`
56	
57	The `--global` surface enables machine-wide installs so operators can share a
58	single skill across every project on the machine without per-project setup.
59	State for global installs is recorded in a separate global state file
60	(`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/installed.json`) and does not pollute per-project
61	state. The `ddx plugin list --global` and `ddx plugin show <name> --global`
62	commands enumerate from the global state.
63	
64	Unmanaged legacy home-directory skill installs (those not created by DDx's
65	`--global` surface) are retired and should not be created manually.
66	
67	## Consequences
68	
69	- `ddx doctor` can report the project install and the global fallback as
70	  distinct states.
71	- Operators can distinguish `ok`, `missing`, and
72	  `lazy-resolves-to-global` for the project layer.
73	- The docs can point agents and operators to the same project-local surfaces
74	  instead of describing competing home-directory and project-directory
75	  locations.
76	- The default package remains available offline through the embedded layer.
77	- `ddx install --global` satisfies the common case where an operator wants a
78	  skill on every project without repeating the install per repository.
79	
80

1	---
2	name: ddx
3	description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "what's blocking the queue", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
4	---
5	
6	# DDx
7	
8	DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
9	document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
10	items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
11	forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness, provider, model,
12	and profile while power travels as `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds;
13	Fizeau owns concrete routing, provider/model discovery, aliases,
14	fuzzy matching, catalog lookups, and transcript/session rendering), a
15	persona system (bindable AI personalities), a library registry
16	(plugins with prompts, templates, personas), and git-aware
17	synchronization. This skill makes any skills-compatible coding agent
18	(Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) understand and operate
19	the DDx surface correctly.
20	
21	## How this skill works
22	
23	The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
24	router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.
25	
26	Install locations: this skill is resolved from the project-local plugin layer
27	(`.ddx/plugins/ddx/`), the global machine layer
28	(`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`), or the binary's baked-in
29	default — see **Install topology** below. `ddx doctor` reports which layer is
30	active.
31	
32	**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
33	matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
34	not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
35	guidance, not this overview alone.**
36	
37	## Install topology
38	
39	DDx resolves the default `ddx` package in three layers: project-local
40	`.ddx/plugins/ddx/`, then the global fallback at
41	`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`, then the baked-in package
42	embedded in the binary. `ddx doctor` reports the project and global
43	layers separately so operators can tell whether the project copy is
44	real, missing, or lazily resolving to the global layer.
45	
46	For project-local installs, the agent-facing skill outputs live in
47	`<project>/.agents/skills/<name>/` and `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>/`.
48	For machine-wide installs (`ddx install <name> --global`), the plugin lands
49	in `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` and skill links are created
50	under `~/.agents/skills/<name>/` and `~/.claude/skills/<name>/`. Manual
51	home-directory skill placements outside these managed paths are retired; use
52	`ddx install --global` instead.
53	
54	## Vocabulary
55	
56	Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
57	exact definitions.
58	
59	- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
60	  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
61	- **Queue** — the set of active beads. The *execution-ready queue* is
62	  `status=open` work whose dependencies are closed and which is not parked by
63	  cooldown, supersession, or execution-eligibility policy. `ddx bead ready`,
64	  `ddx bead status`.
65	- **Lifecycle status** — the persisted bead state: `proposed`, `open`,
66	  `in_progress`, `blocked`, `closed`, or `cancelled`.
67	- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
68	  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
69	- **Blocked** — `status=blocked` work paused by a hard external recheckable
70	  blocker. Ordinary dependency waits are derived queue state, not blocked
71	  status. `ddx bead blocked`.
72	- **Proposed** — work awaiting operator decision before autonomous execution.
73	  Proposed beads are not worker-eligible until accepted, rewritten, split,
74	  blocked externally, cancelled, or otherwise resolved.
75	- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
76	  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
77	- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
78	  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
79	  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
80	- **Run** — one task invocation atom. `ddx run` calls Fizeau `Execute`
81	  once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
82	  raw passthrough constraints.
83	- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
84	  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
85	  finalization.
86	- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
87	  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
88	- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
89	  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
90	- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
91	  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
92	  below).
93	- **Harness** — a Fizeau routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
94	  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
95	  branch on harness names.
96	- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
97	  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
98	  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
99	- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
100	  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
101	  personas.
102	- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
103	  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
104	- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
105	  upstream execution service. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries;
106	  Fizeau owns concrete route selection within those bounds.
107	- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
108	  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
109	  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
110	  `ddx install <name>`.
111	- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
112	  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
113	  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
114	- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
115	  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
116	  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
117	  orchestration; that remains harness business.
118	- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
119	  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
120	- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
121	  `ddx upgrade`.
122	- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
123	  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
124	  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
125	  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
126	- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
127	  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
128	  via `spec-id`.
129	- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
130	  at its governing artifact.
131	
132	## Intent router
133	
134	Before responding, read the matching file.
135	
136	| User says / asks about | Read this file |
137	|---|---|
138	| "what should I work on next", "what's blocking the queue", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", broad queue orientation or planning | `reference/interactive.md` |
139	| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
140	| `ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "start the worker", "start N workers and watch", "monitor the drain", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
141	| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
142	| "assess bead readiness", "score a bead", "triage a failed attempt", "refine a bead", bead authoring lint | `bead-lifecycle/` |
143	| "run an agent", "dispatch", harness/provider/model passthrough, power, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
144	| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |
145	
146	If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
147	run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
148	user which concept they mean rather than guessing.
149	
150	## Top-level policy reminders
151	
152	These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
153	reference file; do not violate them.
154	
155	- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
156	  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
157	  bead history and cannot be audited.
158	- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
159	  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
160	  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
161	  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
162	- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
163	  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
164	  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
165	  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
166	  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
167	  branches.
168	- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
169	  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
170	  its own isolated checkout. `ddx try` does this automatically;
171	  manual parallel work should too.
172	- **Power-first execution dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
173	  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
174	  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.
175	
176	## Links out
177	
178	- Full CLI reference: `ddx --help`, `ddx <subcommand> --help`.
179	- Governing feature specs: see `FEAT-*` documents under your
180	  project's `docs/` tree — especially the CLI, beads, agent-service,
181	  executions, and skills features.
182	- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
183	  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
184	- Open standard this skill conforms to:
185	  [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io).
186

1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Project Overview
6	
7	DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is the shared infrastructure platform for document-driven development. It provides the tools that developers and workflow systems (like HELIX) use to manage the documents AI agents consume to build software.
8	
9	DDx is one layer in a three-project stack:
10	- **DDx** (this repo) — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent service, personas, templates, git sync
11	 - **DDx** (this repo) — platform services: document library, bead tracker, execution primitives, personas, templates, git sync
12	- **HELIX** (`~/Projects/helix`) — workflow methodology: phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, bounded actions
13	- **Dun** (`~/Projects/dun`) — quality check runner: check discovery, execution, agent-friendly output
14	
15	## Architecture
16	
17	This monorepo produces three artifacts:
18	- **`ddx` CLI** (`/cli/`): Go binary — document library mgmt, bead tracker, `run`/`try`/`work`, personas, templates, git sync
19	- **`ddx-server`** (planned): Web server + MCP endpoints for document and bead access
20	- **`ddx.github.io`** (`/website/`): Hugo promotional site
21	
22	### Key Components
23	
24	- `cli/` - Go CLI application source code
25	  - `cmd/` - Cobra command implementations
26	  - `internal/` - Internal packages (config, bead, persona, git, mcp, metaprompt, etc.)
27	  - `main.go` - Application entry point
28	- `website/` - Hugo site with Hextra theme
29	- `library/` - DDx default plugin source (installed to `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` by `ddx init`).
30	  `ddx install <name>` supports two install modes:

1	# Task Dispatch — Power, Persona, Passthrough
2	
3	> **Debugging routing?** Routing lives entirely in Fizeau. See `CONTRACT-003-fizeau-service` (in the agent repo, `docs/helix/02-design/contracts/`) for the public surface; routing internals are inside that module.
4	
5	DDx invokes task execution through the upstream Fizeau service. DDx does not
6	choose harnesses, providers, endpoints, or models on the normal work path. It
7	describes the work, forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness,
8	provider, model, and profile unchanged, requests an abstract power
9	level, and lets Fizeau route. Fizeau owns concrete routing,
10	provider/model discovery, alias resolution, fuzzy matching of raw model
11	strings, transcript/session rendering, and all route-side errors.
12	
13	## Power bounds
14	
15	Normal execution should use power bounds:
16	
17	```bash
18	ddx run --min-power 10 --prompt task.md
19	```
20	
21	Power is an integer scale exposed by the Fizeau contract. DDx passes
22	`MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` bounds and treats them as ordering
23	constraints, not as model identities. DDx does not query a model catalog,
24	discover providers, or pin concrete routes on the normal work path. Fizeau
25	reports what it actually used:
26	
27	```text
28	running with qwen 3.6-27b (power 10)
29	```
30	
31	DDx owns bead retry policy. It decides whether an attempt succeeded using DDx
32	evidence: commits, no-changes rationale, merge/preserve result, gates, review
33	verdicts, and cooldowns. If a retry is allowed, DDx may raise `MinPower`.
34	
35	Fizeau owns route selection within those requested bounds. DDx may raise
36	`MinPower` on retries, but it must not pin a concrete model/provider on the
37	normal work path or infer route selection from a catalog.
38	
39	## Passthrough constraints
40	
41	`--harness`, `--provider`, `--model`, and `--profile` are
42	allowed only as passthrough constraints:
43	
44	```bash
45	ddx run --min-power 10 --model qwen36 --prompt task.md
46	```
47	
48	DDx sends these values to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate them,
49	score them, use them for queue policy, rewrite them on retry, or use them as a
50	fallback mechanism. Fizeau owns any fuzzy matching, alias resolution, provider
51	fallback, or typed error for those raw strings. DDx stops on hard-pin
52	exhaustion; it does not remove pins, widen pins, call route-selection helpers,
53	or retry in a loop.
54	
55	## Execution layering
56	
57	The intended command layering is:
58	
59	- `ddx run` — one Fizeau `Execute` call with requested `MinPower`/`MaxPower`.
60	  No route-selection helper.
61	- `ddx try <bead>` — one bead attempt layered over `run`.
62	- `ddx work` — queue drain and retry policy layered over `try`.
63	
64	Route diagnostics are status/debug-only. Do not use diagnostic route decisions
65	in `run`, `try`, or `work`, and never feed one back into `Execute`.
66	
67	## Personas
68	
69	Personas are reusable AI personality templates. DDx injects a
70	persona's body as a system-prompt addendum to `ddx run` —
71	same persona, same behavior across every harness, because the
72	harness sees a flat system prompt, not a persona file.
73	
74	### Default roster
75	
76	The default `ddx` plugin ships five role-focused personas:
77	
78	- `code-reviewer` — security-first review with structured verdicts
79	- `test-engineer` — stubs-over-mocks, real-e2e, baselined
80	  performance, testing pyramid as shape not ratios
81	- `implementer` — YAGNI / KISS / DOWITYTD, ships tests with code
82	- `architect` — opinionated on when to reach for each pattern
83	  (monolith-first, data-model-first)
84	- `specification-enforcer` — refuses drift from governing artifacts
85	
86	See `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md` for the quality bar and
87	authoring guidance. Projects can install additional personas via
88	plugins.
89	
90	### Using a persona
91	
92	Directly at invocation time:
93	
94	```bash
95	ddx run --persona code-reviewer --prompt review.md
96	```
97	
98	Or by binding a role in `.ddx/config.yaml`:
99	
100	```yaml
101	persona_bindings:
102	  code-reviewer: code-reviewer
103	  test-engineer: test-engineer
104	  architect: architect
105	```
106	
107	Workflows then reference the role abstractly (e.g., "dispatch to
108	the `code-reviewer` role"), and DDx resolves the binding at
109	dispatch time.
110	
111	```bash
112	ddx persona list                # available personas
113	ddx persona show <name>         # persona body + frontmatter
114	ddx persona bind <role> <name>  # set a binding
115	ddx persona bindings            # current role → persona map
116	```
117	
118	## Quorum
119	
120	For multi-agent review or adversarial checks, use a workflow skill composition
121	such as `compare-prompts`, covered in `reference/review.md`:
122	
123	```bash
124	ddx run --persona code-reviewer --prompt review.md
125	ddx run --persona architect --prompt review.md
126	```
127	
128	## Getting more capabilities
129	
130	`ddx install <name>` adds plugins to the project. Plugins can ship
131	personas, prompts, patterns, templates, and workflow skills.
132	
133	```bash
134	ddx install helix              # HELIX workflow plugin
135	ddx search <term>              # discover available plugins
136	ddx installed                  # list installed plugins
137	ddx uninstall <name>           # remove
138	```
139	
140	Custom personas go in `.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/personas/<name>.md`
141	(or directly in `library/personas/<name>.md` for local-only use).
142	See the personas README for the authoring quality bar.
143	
144	## Install topology
145	
146	DDx resolves package installs in this order: project-local
147	`.ddx/plugins/<name>/`, then the global fallback at
148	`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/`, then the baked-in
149	default package for `ddx` itself. `ddx doctor` exposes the project and
150	global `ddx` layers separately so operators can distinguish a real
151	project install from a global fallback.
152	
153	The agent-facing skill outputs are the project-local `.agents/skills/`
154	and `.claude/skills/` directories. Those are the install targets for the
155	resolved package; home-directory skill installs are retired.
156	
157	## Anti-patterns
158	
159	- **Stacking power bounds and passthrough pins casually**. Normal DDx work
160	  should request `MinPower`. Use passthrough pins only for explicit operator
161	  constraints; DDx must not interpret them.
162	- **Hardcoding a harness name in a workflow**. Workflow should
163	  dispatch by power or role binding; letting each project route through its
164	  agent configuration is the point of DDx's agent boundary.
165	- **Persona for everything**. Personas add a system-prompt addendum;
166	  they don't make a bad prompt good. Start with a clear prompt;
167	  reach for a persona when you want consistent style/standards
168	  across invocations.
169	- **Persona files in skill directories**. Personas live in
170	  `library/personas/` or `.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/personas/`, not in
171	  `.claude/skills/` or `.agents/skills/`. Don't mix the two.
172	
173	## CLI reference
174	
175	```bash
176	# Dispatch
177	ddx run --min-power 10 --prompt task.md
178	ddx run --top-power --prompt task.md
179	ddx run --min-power 10 --max-power 20 --prompt task.md
180	ddx run --min-power 10 --provider openrouter --model qwen36 --profile default --prompt task.md
181	ddx run --persona code-reviewer --prompt review.md
182	
183	# Introspection
184	ddx doctor                              # environment health
185	ddx status                              # project drift / stale docs
186	ddx bead status                         # queue state
187	# Fizeau diagnostics, if exposed by the project, remain separate from DDx verbs.
188	
189	# Personas
190	ddx persona list
191	ddx persona show <name>
192	ddx persona bind <role> <persona>
193	ddx persona bindings
194	
195	# Plugin install
196	ddx install <plugin>
197	ddx search <term>
198	ddx installed
199	ddx uninstall <name>
200	```
201	
202	Full flag list: `ddx run --help`, `ddx persona --help`,
203	`ddx install --help`.
204

29:- `library/` - DDx default plugin source (installed to `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` by `ddx init`).
30:  `ddx install <name>` supports two install modes:
34:  - **Global** (`--global`): writes to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/`; skill

210:## Persona System
220:Plugin and persona lookup follows a three-layer precedence: project-local (`<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`) → global (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/`) → baked-in binary default (only for the `ddx` plugin). The project layer always wins when present. `ddx doctor` reports both the global install layer and the project install layer, including when the project copy is absent and falls through to the global layer (`lazy-resolves-to-global`). Use `ddx install <name> --global` to install machine-wide (skills land in `~/.claude/skills/` and `~/.agents/skills/`); omit `--global` for per-project installs. See `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md` for the full decision record.

data.text: Let me check the actual Persona System section:

data.output:
210	## Persona System
211	
212	DDX includes a persona system that provides consistent AI personalities for different roles:
213	
214	- **Personas**: Reusable AI personality templates (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`, `architect`)
215	- **Roles**: Abstract functions that personas fulfill (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`)
216	- **Bindings**: Project-specific mappings between roles and personas in `.ddx.yml`
217	
218	Personas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.
219	
220	Plugin and persona lookup follows a three-layer precedence: project-local (`<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`) → global (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/`) → baked-in binary default (only for the `ddx` plugin). The project layer always wins when present. `ddx doctor` reports both the global install layer and the project install layer, including when the project copy is absent and falls through to the global layer (`lazy-resolves-to-global`). Use `ddx install <name> --global` to install machine-wide (skills land in `~/.claude/skills/` and `~/.agents/skills/`); omit `--global` for per-project installs. See `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md` for the full decision record.
221	
222	## When filing beads
223	
224	Bead descriptions + AC must be sufficient context for a sub-agent to execute the work without operator hand-curation (principle P7, `docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`). Before invoking `ddx bead create`, walk the 8-criterion rubric in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md`: title clarity, root cause with file:line, numbered AC with specific test names, wired-in assertion, `cd cli && go test` + lefthook gate, labels with cross-refs, parent + deps, standalone description (no `/tmp/*`, no chat references). Beads scoring < 7/8 will fail in autonomous drain.
225	
226	See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.
227	
228	## Execution Evidence Convention
229	
230	`.ddx/executions/` is **per-machine working state** — it is listed in `.gitignore` and is NOT committed to git. Files written there by agents (prompt.md, result.json, manifest.json, etc.) exist only on the local machine. The durable audit trail is the events stream in `.ddx/attachments/`, not the execution dirs.
231	
232	When a bead's acceptance criteria asks for an investigation report, findings document, or any other freestanding non-source artifact, the agent must write that artifact under the per-attempt evidence directory `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` (the `{{.AttemptDir}}` slot in execute-bead prompts). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository working directory** — such paths are invisible to the post-execution reviewer and do not survive between machines. If the bead names a specific tracked in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/<short-name>.md` (which will be on-disk but untracked).
233	
234	## When filing beads

commit 9e748db9cd5796cc288be83d37ecf6f199927b8c
Author: ddx-checkpoint <checkpoint@ddx.local>
Date:   Wed Jun 3 18:59:20 2026 -0400

    docs: update ADR-027 and CLAUDE.md Persona System for --global install surface [ddx-6a23cbd3]
    
    ADR-027 previously stated home-directory skill installs are retired and DDx
    does not add new state under ~/.agents/skills or ~/.claude/skills. This
    contradicts the --global install surface implemented in the sibling beads.
    
    Update both docs to reflect the two-mode install topology:
    - Project-local (default): plugin under <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/,
      skills linked into <project>/.agents/skills/ and <project>/.claude/skills/
    - Global (--global): plugin under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/,
      skills linked into ~/.agents/skills/ and ~/.claude/skills/; recorded in a
      separate global state file; inspectable via ddx plugin list/show --global
    
    Unmanaged legacy home-directory skill installs remain retired.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
